message:
feat(io,websocket): capability discovery and RFC 6455 extension negotiation; fix(mpsc): deadlock on receiver drop

io/cap.rs: Replace TODO stubs with concrete capability-discovery and
instrumentation APIs:
- New `IoCapabilities` struct with `file_ops`, `network_ops`,
  `timer_integration`, `deterministic` booleans and a `const LAB` preset
- New `IoStats` struct tracking `submitted`/`completed` operation counts
- `IoCap` trait gains `capabilities()` (required) and `stats()` (defaulted)
- `LabIoCap` now holds `AtomicU64` counters with `record_submit()` and
  `record_complete()` methods for lock-free instrumentation
- Tests updated to verify capabilities and stats tracking

net/websocket/handshake.rs: Implement proper RFC 6455 extension negotiation:
- Fix wire separator from "; " (incorrect) to ", " (correct per RFC 6455 §9.1)
  in both client request builder and server response serializer
- New `parse_extension_offers()` and `extension_token()` helpers
- Client-side: validate_response now checks Sec-WebSocket-Extensions header and
  rejects unsolicited extensions with new `HandshakeError::ExtensionMismatch`
- Server-side: accept() parses client offers, filters against
  supported_extensions (case-insensitive), deduplicates via BTreeSet, returns
  negotiated extensions preserving original parameters
- Three new tests covering server negotiation, unsolicited rejection, and
  successful acceptance

channel/mpsc.rs: Fix potential deadlock when dropping Receiver. Previously,
`queue.clear()` was called while holding the inner mutex, meaning all queued T
items were dropped under the lock. If T::drop interacts with the same channel
(e.g., dropping the last Sender clone triggers mutex acquisition), this
deadlocks. Now uses `mem::take()` to move the queue out, explicitly drops the
lock guard, then lets the extracted queue drop outside the critical section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
